samples.xml_description:
<module id="edge_detect">
    <description>Implement a module for edge detection.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="a">Input signal.</input>
        <output id="rise">Output signal indicating a rising edge.</output>
        <output id="down">Output signal indicating a falling edge.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="state" width_description="[1:0]">
            <enum-value id="ZERO" description="State ZERO">0</enum-value>
            <enum-value id="ONE" description="State ONE">1</enum-value>
        </enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" width_description="[1:0]">
            <reg-value id="state">State of the edge detection.</reg-value>
        </reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
    The edge_detect module detects rising and falling edges in the input signal a and generates corresponding output signals rise and down. 
    The module uses a finite state machine (FSM) with two states: ZERO and ONE. 
    In the ZERO state, the input signal a is 0, and in the ONE state, the input signal a is 1. 
    The module detects a rising edge when the state changes from ZERO to ONE and a falling edge when the state changes from ONE to ZERO.
    The module has three always blocks: 
    - The first always block is a sequential logic block that updates the state of the FSM on the positive edge of the clock signal clk. 
    - The second always block is a combinational logic block that determines the next state of the FSM based on the current state and the input signal a. 
    - The third always block is a sequential logic block that updates the output signals rise and down on the positive edge of the clock signal clk. 
    The output signal rise is set to 1 when the state changes from ZERO to ONE, and the output signal down is set to 1 when the state changes from ONE to ZERO. 
    Otherwise, both output signals are set to 0.
    </implementation>
</module>
